notes_md:
**với** usually means **“with”** — together with someone or something. It connects two people or things in the same action.

It comes **before** the noun/pronoun it connects.  

It can also mean **“and”** when listing things in a more spoken, natural way (cơm với cá).  

Memory tip: if English uses “with,” Vietnamese often uses **với**.
notes:
với usually means “with” — together with someone or something. It connects two people or things in the same action.

It comes before the noun/pronoun it connects.  

It can also mean “and” when listing things in a more spoken, natural way (cơm với cá).  

Memory tip: if English uses “with,” Vietnamese often uses với.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I eat rice with my family.
sentenceBreakdowns: Chị (she) + uống (drinks) + trà (tea) + với (with) + anh ấy (him)
Em (I) + ăn (eat) + cơm (rice) + với (with) + gia đình (family)
exampleSentences: Chị uống trà với anh ấy.
Em ăn cơm với gia đình.

definitions.vi: Một giới từ được sử dụng để chỉ sự đồng hành hoặc liên kết.
definitions.es: Una preposición utilizada para indicar compañía o asociación.
definitions.en: A preposition used to indicate companionship or association.
